title: hymenopappus filifolius var. idahoensis
intro: Hymenopappus filifolius var. idahoensis, commonly known as Idaho fleabane, is a perennial plant species that belongs to the daisy family (Asteraceae). The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating wounds, skin irritations, and infections. The health benefits of Idaho fleabane include its ability to reduce inflammation, soothe skin conditions, and promote wound healing. Idaho fleabane can be prepared for medicinal use through herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, and it is often used in combination with other herbs for enhanced therapeutic effects.
